titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in the Soviet Union ( (Smith) ) to the Acting Secretary of State

div.head: The Ambassador in the Soviet Union ( Smith ) to the Acting Secretary of State
div.p: 844. Personal for Stuart from Marshall. Please cable following report to Moscow for my personal information: (a) summary of situation, political and military, including estimate of National Army state of supply, (b) recommendation for guidance of State Department as to action, if any, to be taken at this time.

I would like the foregoing to be primarily your personal estimate and views, to be followed by any important difference [of] views, including estimates on state of National Army so far as known from Generals LucasMaj. Gen. John P. Lucas, Chief of the U. S. Army Advisory Group. and McConnell.Brig. Gen. John P. McConnell, Director of the Air Division, Army Advisory Group. Also, if conveniently and confidentially obtainable, I would like a summary of Durdin’sTillman Durdin, New York Times correspondent in China. estimate and recommendation and also Art Steele’s,Arch T. Steele, New York Herald Tribune correspondent in China. if he is in Nanking or China.

Label your reply for my personal attention.In telegram No. 845, March 16, 7 p.m., Secretary of State Marshall reported from Moscow he had received Ambassador Stuart’s telegram No. 519, March 11, 4 p.m., p. 499, and considered it a partial answer to this request.
Department please pass to Nanking for action as Moscow’s 2.Repeated to Nanking on March 16, 11 a.m. [Marshall.]
